This is interesting - now I can see the hooks.json configuration for the claude-mem plugin. This shows the hooks that were configured:

1. Setup hook - runs setup.sh
2. SessionStart hook - runs smart-install.js and worker-service.cjs
3. UserPromptSubmit hook - runs session-init
4. PostToolUse hook - runs observation
5. PreToolUse hook - runs file-context (only for Read tool)
6. Stop hook - runs summarize
7. SessionEnd hook - runs session-complete

The user wants to temporarily disable this and remove the hooks. This is useful information about what the claude-mem system does through its hooks.
